blurb:
Sunnyside is the kind of neighborhood pizza place you keep coming back to without really needing a reason. The vibe is casual and easy, and it works great for a family dinner, a Friday night with friends, or just picking up a pie on the way home.

The Hot Honey Charred pizza gets ordered a lot, and the Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are the real sleeper hit as a starter. The Caesar salad is simple and done right if you want something fresh on the side.

They're closed on Mondays, so keep that in mind for the start of your week.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks is a solid, no-frills Chinese spot right on Roswell Road that's been a reliable option in the neighborhood for a while. The menu goes a little beyond the usual takeout fare, which is nice. They do hot pot, BBQ duck, and lobster in black bean sauce if you want something more adventurous.

It works well for a casual family dinner or when you just want Chinese food without making it a whole thing. The portions are reasonable and they're open late most nights.

Open every day until 10pm, which is handy when you need a late dinner option.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Heirloom Market BBQ is a small, no-frills spot off Akers Mill Road that a lot of people in this area consider the best BBQ in Atlanta. There's one communal table inside and the place is tiny, but that's kind of the point. This is about the food, and it draws a crowd. Good for a weekday lunch when you want something memorable.

What makes it different from your average BBQ joint is the Korean influence you'll notice in some of the flavors and sides. It's BBQ, but with a little more going on. They also have burgers if someone in your group isn't feeling smoked meat.

They're only open Tuesday through Saturday, 11am to 8pm, and they're closed Sunday and Monday. Get there early because they do sell out.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Los Bravos is a reliable neighborhood Mexican spot that's been around long enough to have a real following. The food is consistent and the portions are solid, which is why people keep coming back. Good for a casual family dinner or lunch with coworkers.

The kitchen is where they shine, so come hungry and order food. Long-time regulars say the kitchen delivers, and the price is easy on the wallet for a sit-down meal.

One honest heads up from regulars: the margarita prices have crept up noticeably and can be a bit of a shock compared to other spots nearby. Stick to the food and you'll leave happy.
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
SATE is a little gem on Roswell Road that I think a lot of people drive past without stopping. The owner is genuinely welcoming and takes real pride in what they're doing. If you've never had pho before, this is actually a great place to try it for the first time.

They make their pho the traditional way, which you can taste. It's a warm, filling bowl and the portions are generous for the price. This is a great spot for a quick lunch or an easy weeknight dinner when you want something comforting.

They're open every day from 10am to 9pm, which makes it pretty convenient.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
J. Christopher's is our go-to for a solid weekend breakfast that doesn't require a long wait or a special occasion. It's a relaxed daytime spot in Sandy Springs that does classic breakfast and lunch well without any fuss. Good for a slow Saturday morning or grabbing lunch with a coworker.

They're known for their omelets and rotate seasonal specials, so there's usually something a little different to try beyond the usual. The burgers at lunch are worth a look too if you're not in a breakfast mood.

They close at 2pm every day, so this is strictly a morning and midday spot. Plan accordingly.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you're looking for a solid, no-fuss pizza spot on Roswell Road, JJ's is the one. It's casual and affordable, the kind of place you call when the whole family is hungry and nobody wants to overthink dinner. Good for a weeknight pickup or a lazy Friday night in.

The stuffed pizza is what people are raving about. Reviewers say one slice is basically a full meal, and the value is hard to beat. If you haven't tried it yet, that's the move.

Just a heads up that they're closed on Tuesdays. Everything else is pretty straightforward, and delivery is available if you don't feel like going out.

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is a genuine find. It's a Brazilian-style acai shop where you build your own bowl at the self-serve bar, piling on as many toppings as you want. Fun for the kids, great for a post-workout treat, and honestly just a nice change of pace from the usual options around here.

The acai bowls are the main event, but they also have crepes and coxinha, which is a popular Brazilian fried chicken snack. The staff gets high marks from pretty much everyone who walks through the door.

They're closed on Tuesdays, and they're open until 10pm most nights which makes them a good option when you want something a little different for a late snack.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Ray's on the River is the kind of place you save for a real occasion. It sits right on the Chattahoochee off Powers Ferry Road, and the view alone is worth the trip. This is where you take out-of-town guests, celebrate an anniversary, or just treat yourselves when you want a proper night out.

The menu leans into Southern seafood, and the setting is intimate without being stuffy. It's been a go-to for special dinners in this area for years and the 4.6 rating across over 11,000 reviews says everything.

Sunday hours are split, with a break between 4pm and 5pm, so plan accordingly if you're going for an early Sunday dinner.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Royal Spice is where we head when we're in the mood for Indian and want a real sit-down meal without driving far. The staff is genuinely helpful, especially if you're not sure what to order or have questions about the dishes. It's a good spot for a dinner out with family or a low-key date night.

The entrees are flavorful and the appetizers are worth ordering, not just skipping past. The servers will help you figure out what to try if you ask.

Just note they close between lunch and dinner on weekdays, so there's a gap between 2:30pm and 4:30pm where you can't walk in.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
